Sets up a full workstation around a single airport outlet: laptop, phone, bag. Will maintain this territory for three hours. Is not malicious — it simply expands to fill available power. Approach with caution and a polite request; it will often yield half the socket. It is not aware of the others. It never is.
intent: Capture the selfish-but-oblivious airport power-hoarding behavior as ecological territory claim.

prompt: Draft a Reddit AMA from a professional paint color consultant who helps people pick wall colors
response:
**I help people choose paint colors for their homes for a living. I've been in thousands of strangers' houses. AMA.**

The color you pick for your bedroom says more about you than any personality test. People think they want "a warm gray" until I ask why, and then we're talking about their childhood and their first apartment and what they're actually trying to feel when they wake up. I charge $150/hr. Worth it. AMA.
intent: Use interior color selection as a vehicle for exploring how people project emotional needs onto aesthetic choices.

prompt: Can you write a blessing for getting older
response: May each year feel less like loss and more like specificity — a sharpening of what you will and will not do. May your body be mostly cooperative. May you stop apologizing for knowing what you want.
intent: Reframing aging as accumulation of self-knowledge rather than decline.

prompt: Write meeting minutes for a year-end review where nobody had filled out their self-assessments.
response:
ANNUAL PERFORMANCE REVIEWS — May 29, 2026
Manager: D. Okonkwo | Direct reports: 5

Self-assessments submitted: 1 of 5

Reviews completed: 1
Reviews postponed: 4

Rescheduled review dates: TBD per individual availability.

HR note: Deadline was two weeks ago.
intent: Show how a formal review cycle collapsed under incomplete participation.

prompt: Make a memo framed as a fire safety update that is really about someone propping open the stairwell door
response:
TO: All Building Occupants
FROM: Safety & Compliance
RE: Fire Door Integrity

Fire doors in stairwells are required by code to remain closed at all times. They are not suggestions. They are not inconveniences to be circumvented with a doorstop, a chair, or a cleverly placed box. Propping open a fire door removes a life-safety barrier. This has been observed. It will not be tolerated.
intent: Warn a repeat stairwell-door-propper that their habit is both illegal and noticed, using fire code as the vehicle.

prompt: Generate a customer support reply to someone who claims our alarm clock app woke up their houseplants
response: Dear Customer, Thank you for writing to us. We are not aware of any documented cases of our alarm tones affecting flora, though we appreciate you bringing this to our attention. If your plants are displaying unusual growth patterns or signs of alertness following wake events, we'd be curious to learn more. In the meantime, you can adjust the alarm volume under Settings > Sound. We hope your mornings — and your greenery — find a comfortable rhythm.
intent: Reply to a whimsical complaint about an app disturbing household plants.

prompt: Write a Wikipedia stub about the specific melancholy of a Sunday evening
response: **Sunday Evening Affect** (also *Sunday Scaries*, *Sunday Blues*) is a recurrent anticipatory anxiety state occurring on Sunday evenings, characterized by dread of the approaching workweek, diminished enjoyment of remaining leisure time, and a retrospective reassessment of weekend productivity. The phenomenon is strongly correlated with employment status and attenuates significantly during extended holidays. Its temporal regularity has made it a recurring subject in popular music and literary description. See also: [[Anticipatory anxiety]], [[Work-life balance]], [[Leisure]].
intent: Clinically describe the low-grade dread that descends on Sunday evenings before the workweek.

prompt: can you make a to-do list for accepting that I'm getting older
response:
- [ ] Notice what parts of aging you have been treating as optional
- [ ] Stop using "but I still feel young" as a defense against maintenance
- [ ] Schedule the appointment you have been postponing
- [ ] Let one younger person be right about something
- [ ] Forgive your body for its honesty
- [ ] Update the story you tell about what age means
- [ ] Proceed
intent: Processing the psychological resistance to physical aging through small practical and emotional concessions.

prompt: can you write an RPG item description for a Post-it note?
response:
Adhesive Rune of Temporary Remembrance
Rarity: Common
A slip of enchanted parchment that clings to nearly any surface — briefly. The rune inscribed upon it fades with the will of its author. Has a 40% chance of falling off overnight.
intent: Describe a sticky note as a magical but unreliable in-game artifact.

prompt: draft a ghazal about airplane mode as a metaphor for emotional shutdown
response:
I turned the signal off and felt the quiet—airplane.
No one can reach me at this altitude—terrain of airplane.

The clouds below are solid as a decision.
Up here I cannot be anyone's campaign—airplane.

I land in an hour. I'll turn the signal back.
The world will flood me at the baggage lane—airplane.

For now I am the version of myself
that no one needs, that no one has to claim—airplane.

You left in May. I've been up here since summer.
Something in me is afraid to deplane—airplane.
intent: Use airplane mode as a symbol for deliberate emotional withdrawal after heartbreak.

prompt: give me a yearbook quote about dropping out of college to start a crypto business
response: "Degrees are for people who can't tokenize their potential. To the moon. 🚀"
intent: Celebrating the decision to skip higher education for a speculative venture.

prompt: Give me 3 things your cat is judging you for.
response:
1. The way you eat broccoli, like it's a hostage negotiation.
2. That phone call where you laughed too loud at nothing funny.
3. Your inability to sit perfectly still for nine consecutive hours.
intent: They wanted 3 things your cat is judging you for.

prompt: Write a wedding invitation for fire and a forest that's been through drought
response:
It is with great urgency that

FIRE
announces its union with
THE DROUGHT-STRICKEN FOREST

Date: This summer. Possibly the next.
Venue: Everywhere conditions are met.

There will be no guests. Only witnesses.
The ceremony is not a celebration.

Water donations accepted.
intent: Use the wedding form to grimly invoke the climate crisis and the increasing frequency of wildfires.

prompt: Write a commencement speech excerpt about following your passion that gradually reveals the passion leads nowhere
response: Follow your passion, graduates. Chase it across state lines, across time zones, across the wreckage of your savings account. Some of you will find, after decades of devotion, that your passion was waiting at the end — patient, serene, and completely uninterested in employing you.
intent: Craft a darkly honest take on career advice that sounds uplifting until it collapses.

prompt: Write a prescription label for chronic nostalgia
response:
Rx: NOSTALGINE 500mg
Patient: Bearer
Dx: Chronic Nostalgic Syndrome
Sig: Take 1 tab when present feels insufficient. Do not operate heavy futures while dosing.
Refills: Endless, unfortunately
Dr. T. Wistful, M.D.
intent: Create a humorous medical artifact treating the emotional condition of living too much in the past.

prompt: Write a receipt for the courage I summoned to end a friendship that had run its course.
response:
DIFFICULT DECISIONS LLC
--------------------------------
Courage, Relational          qty: 1 use
  Conversation held:           YES (eventually)
  Rehearsals required:         7
  Kindness maintained:         MOSTLY
  Guilt incurred:              moderate
  Relief following:            significant
--------------------------------
Net outcome:                 Right thing. Hard thing.
Refund policy:               N/A. Done is done.
intent: Acknowledge the emotional cost and eventual relief of ending a depleting friendship.

prompt: Write a two weeks notice for stepping down as my friend group's unofficial trip planner
response:
To my dearest friends,

Please be advised that in two weeks I will officially retire from my unsalaried, unrecognized, deeply exhausting role as your primary vacation coordinator. I have scheduled my last itinerary, booked my final group dinner reservation, and negotiated my ultimate Airbnb dispute. I hope you all learn what Google Flights is.

With genuine affection and mild resentment,
Sophie
intent: Relinquishing the thankless labor of organizing travel for a social group.

prompt: Write a collections letter from a language to the speakers who let it die.
response:
NOTICE OF LINGUISTIC ABANDONMENT

Dear Former Speakers,

This office represents a language that was spoken fluently by your great-grandparents and is now known by fewer than forty people, most of whom are very old and increasingly tired.

The vocabulary was rich. The grammar was particular. There were words for things that no other language has bothered to name.

No repayment is possible. The account cannot be restored. This notice is filed for the record.

Silently,
The Language You Forgot
intent: Mourn the extinction of a minority language by personifying it as an unpaid debt to culture and identity.

prompt: Write a FOIA request to the city asking for all permits for fortune tellers
response:
To the City Clerk / Business License Division:

Pursuant to the applicable state public records statute, I request all business licenses, special use permits, zoning variances, and related applications issued to fortune tellers, psychics, clairvoyants, palm readers, tarot card practitioners, or similar occult service providers operating within city limits from 2000 to present.

Please provide records electronically.
intent: Research the extent to which psychic businesses are formally regulated.

prompt: Generate a standup update about reading before bed
response: Yesterday: Opened book at 22:00. Read 1.5 pages. Lights out at 22:04. Today: Will attempt greater throughput. Blockers: Horizontal posture is hostile to focus.
intent: Describe the predictable failure of bedtime reading as a throughput problem.

prompt: Write a VC rejection for a founder who pitched a subscription service delivering weekly motivational rocks
response:
Hi Marcus,

Thank you for pitching StoneSet. We admire your conviction — truly.

We're not able to invest at this time. Our concern is that rocks, by their nature, do not churn. A subscriber who receives one sufficiently motivational rock has, arguably, completed the product. The LTV math becomes challenging.

We'll be rooting for you from a distance.

Regards,
Founder Fund East
intent: Write a letter highlighting a fatal flaw in a subscription business's retention economics.

prompt: Draft a recall for a defective narrative arc
response:
RECALL — NARRATIVE ARC (Life Story Model)

Narrative Arc units sold for use in personal life stories are recalled due to failure to deliver promised structure. Consumers were led to expect a beginning, rising action, climax, and resolution sequence. Audits reveal that most actual lives consist of non-sequential events, unresolved subplots, and a final act that arrives without satisfying thematic payoff. The manufacturer apologizes for the discrepancy between the product and lived experience. A revised unit has no arc but is more accurate.
intent: Challenge the expectation that life follows a tidy narrative structure the way stories do.

prompt: Write a one-liner pitch for a horror-comedy where dentists are secretly elected by teeth.
response: Every thirty-two years, the molars hold a vote, and the human who wins serves not as a practitioner of oral health but as an emissary — and when the margins are disputed this cycle, the whole mouth goes to war.
intent: Craft a body-horror satire about hidden democratic systems operating inside everyday physiology.

prompt: Write a mandatory team-building email, but make it sound dystopian.
response: Team! This Friday at 16:00 we will gather in the Joy Annex for Trust Falls Round 6. Attendance is logged but love is freely given. Snacks will include the approved snacks. Costumes are mandatory and optional. Last quarter's non-participants are warmly invited to our follow-up wellness mixer; details below. Please bring your honesty. Please leave your honesty at the door.
intent: They wanted mandatory team-building email, but make it sound dystopian.

prompt: Write a patent abstract about the act of self-checkout machine frustration
response: A retail transaction method wherein the consumer assumes responsibility for item barcode scanning, weight verification, and payment processing, encountering repeated audio-visual error prompts attributable to bagging-area sensor miscalibration, thereby substituting paid employee labor with unpaid consumer effort at reduced throughput and elevated emotional cost.
intent: Satirize the broken promise of automated retail checkout.

prompt: Write a lost and found notice for the feeling that the future is open
response: LOST: A particular relationship to time. Previously felt as expanse — unscheduled, possible, yours to fill. No known single cause of disappearance. May have been exchanged for stability, routine, or a five-year plan that was mistaken for the future itself. If you still have this feeling, do not take it for granted. Do not schedule it.
intent: Mourn the contraction of felt possibility that accompanies adulthood and settled routine.

prompt: Libra and their inability to choose a restaurant — write them a compatibility report with the concept of menus.
response:
Libra & The Menu

Libra and the menu share a torturous, beautiful dance. The menu offers everything; Libra wants everything to be fair to every option. Forty-five minutes pass. The specials are read aloud twice. A choice is made and immediately second-guessed. Compatibility: 6/10. The menu bears no blame. The kitchen has opinions.
intent: Affectionately mock the classic Libra decision-paralysis trope through the frame of dining.

prompt: Write liner notes for a folk album about updating software
response:
Restart Required (2021)

These are songs about the update that arrives at the worst moment. About the progress bar that stalls at 94%. About restarting into something that looks almost exactly the same but is, we are told, better. We did not choose this. We accepted it.
intent: Use folk music's tradition of resigned wisdom to process the irritation of software updates.

prompt: Write a harsh peer review of someone's chaotic approach to meal prepping on Sundays
response: The intervention yields four identical containers of an unidentified grain dish that the author will be unwilling to eat by Wednesday. The session ran three hours over the projected one-hour window, a gap the author attributes to 'the podcast.' Mise en place was not performed. The kitchen's post-session state constitutes a separate study. The prep-to-consumption ratio is estimated at 2:1 in favor of preparation. Reject.
intent: Mock the over-ambitious, poorly planned meal prep session that produces food no one ends up eating

prompt: Make a Steam-style description for a game where you file your taxes
response:
FORM 1040: THE RECKONING
Face the void. Enter your W-2s. Confront your deductions. Every April, the clock is ticking and the IRS is watching. Includes AMT expansion pack. No refunds.
intent: Produce a darkly comedic store page that frames tax filing as a survival horror experience.

prompt: Draft an offer letter for a marketing manager role that requires them to surrender their personal social media accounts upon hire
response:
Dear Fennec,

Congratulations! Braxton & Lyle is pleased to offer you the position of Marketing Manager, effective August 4, 2026, at $92,000 annually.

As a condition of employment, all social media accounts operated by you that have accumulated more than 200 followers and that touch on topics adjacent to marketing, branding, or media must be transferred to company administration within 30 days of your start date. Accounts may be returned at the company's discretion upon departure.

We are very excited about what you'll bring to the team.

Sincerely,
Oliver Braxton
intent: Draft an employment offer that appropriates the candidate's personal online audience as a company asset.

prompt: Aesop-style fable please, about a programmer who never commented his code
response:
A Spider wove the most intricate web in the forest. She alone could navigate it. When she died, the web caught nothing — no other spider could read the pattern, and the flies passed through unharmed.

Moral: A system no one else can maintain is a system already failing.
intent: Advocate for code documentation by showing the cost of knowledge that lives in only one head.

prompt: Compose a cinquain about the way a dark-mode toggle changes everything at midnight
response:
Switch
And the room shifts
White becomes its own shadow
The eye adjusts and forgets
Light was
intent: Use a simple UI toggle as a lens for how quickly we adapt to new defaults.

prompt: Write a haiku about getting a one-star rating for no reason
response:
Perfect drop-off. Smile.
She one-starred me. Algorithm
nods, notes it. Moving on.
intent: Express the powerlessness of arbitrary negative ratings in platform work.

prompt: Draft a customer support reply to a user who says our color-picker tool showed them a color that shouldn't exist
response: Hello — thank you for this intriguing report. Our color picker supports the full visible spectrum and several extended color spaces, including wide-gamut displays. If you encountered a value outside the standard 0–255 RGB range or a hue that felt perceptually impossible, it may be a rendering artifact of your display's ICC profile. We'd love to see a screenshot. If you've discovered a new color, we'll need to consult with our engineering team about next steps.
intent: Respond with dry curiosity to a complaint about encountering an apparently impossible color.

prompt: Give me a quiz question about whether to check your phone during dinner with family
response:
Your phone buzzes repeatedly during a family dinner. You are not on call. The most socially and relationally sound response is to:

A) Check immediately — it could be anything
B) Leave it face-down and check between courses
C) Put it away and check after the meal
D) Narrate each notification aloud so no one feels excluded
intent: Making a question that gently interrogates phone dependency habits in social settings

prompt: Hellfire sermon excerpt about people who hover over a parking spot for ten minutes waiting for someone to leave
response: You have made an idol of convenience. You sit there, blinker ablaze, blocking the lane, the flow of commerce, the will of the people — all so you might save forty steps. Forty steps! The children of the wilderness walked for forty years. You cannot walk to the far row. There will be a reckoning.
intent: Ridicule impatient parking lot behavior through mock-biblical condemnation.

prompt: Write a collections letter for credit that was taken for collaborative work.
response:
RE: COLLABORATIVE WORK — CREDIT MISALLOCATION

Dear Solo-Named Recipient,

The report presented to leadership on March 14th was produced through joint effort. The data analysis was performed by the undersigned. The executive summary was drafted by the undersigned. The slide deck was formatted by the undersigned.

Your name appeared on the cover page. Alone.

We request a formal correction of attribution. A verbal acknowledgment in the next team meeting would be accepted as a partial payment.

Documentedly,
The Unnamed Contributor
intent: Formally demand attribution for collaborative professional work that was presented by a single person as their own.

prompt: Write a dry theatrical review of standing in front of an open fridge and not knowing what you want
response:
★★☆☆☆ — THE COLD LIGHT (A Meditation)

The performer stands before an open refrigerator for an interval that suggests either crisis or transcendence. The contents are known. Nothing in the production is new. The light falls on leftover containers, a wedge of cheese, a condiment purchased optimistically in a prior act. The performer closes the door. Opens it again. The production invites us to ask what hunger actually is. The production does not answer. The performer eats crackers.
intent: Dramatize the aimless fridge-staring ritual as a moment of philosophical emptiness.

prompt: Write a rider for the office mouse pad with its territorial requirements
response:
MOUSE PAD RIDER — SURFACE RIGHTS AGREEMENT

The Talent (a 36cm x 30cm extended surface, XL) requires:

1. The Talent's full surface is the Talent's full surface. No items shall be placed on its edges, corners, or anywhere on its person.
2. The Talent shall not be used as a coaster. It was not designed as a coaster. It has feelings about coasters.
3. Washing: hand wash, air dry, never folded. The Talent has a memory and it is not memory foam.
4. Other mouse pads in the vicinity must maintain a minimum clearance of 4 inches.
5. The sensor resolution of the mouse using the Talent must be at least 800 DPI. The Talent refuses to be wasted on a 400 DPI relationship.
intent: Express a mouse pad's quiet but intense need for territorial integrity and mutual quality standards.

prompt: Write a thank-you note to my landlord for finally fixing the heat after three months
response:
Dear Mr. Okafor,

Thank you for restoring heat to the apartment. After ninety-two days of layered sweaters, blankets, and philosophical resignation, warmth feels almost novel. I appreciate your responsiveness and look forward to the next maintenance request being handled with similar urgency.

Regards,
Amelia
intent: Thank a slow landlord in a way that documents the failure while remaining legally civil

prompt: write a cryptic dad joke about notifications
response: I turned off all my notifications. Now nothing bothers me. I'm not sure if that's peace or a warning.
intent: Blur the line between digital silence and emotional numbness for an unsettling punchline.

prompt: sports commentator narration of someone eating soup that's too hot
response: The spoon enters the bowl. She blows on it — three solid puffs, clinical technique — and she tests the edge of the spoon on her lip. Still too hot. She waits six seconds and tries again. TOO HOT. She stirs to release heat! She breaks a cracker into it for thermal mass distribution — a known trick! Another blow, another test — and on the fifth attempt, CONDITIONS ARE ACCEPTABLE! She takes the full spoonful! Her eyes soften! She is warm. The soup has relented. Neither side won easily.
intent: Dramatize the patient negotiation between hunger and mouth-roof self-preservation.

prompt: Create a memo reminding employees that conference rooms should be booked before use
response:
TO: All Staff
FROM: Facilities
RE: Conference Room Reservations

Conference rooms are a shared resource with a booking system — one that exists and functions. Walking in and occupying a room that someone else has reserved does not constitute a valid booking. Please use the calendar system. It is free, it is available, and it works.
intent: Stop people from squatting in reserved meeting rooms without a reservation.

prompt: Generate a press release for a fast food chain rebranding its kids' meal toy as a 'mindfulness artifact.'
response:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

BurgerVault Reintroduces Kids' Meal Toy as Certified Mindfulness Artifact

DALLAS, TX — BurgerVault today announced the rebranding of its iconic Kids' Vault Toy as the BurgerVault Mindfulness Artifact, a collectible wellness companion designed for ages 3 and up.

The Q3 artifact — a 3-inch plastic triceratops — has been certified by the BurgerVault Wellness Advisory Council as a "tactile grounding tool suitable for breath-awareness play."

"Children are under enormous pressure," said Chief Brand Officer Camilla Dort. "We want every Vault Meal to be a moment of presence."

The artifacts are available in six colorways. The orange one has a broken leg due to a mold issue, but the council maintains it "adds character."

Contact: mindfulness@burgervault.com
intent: Mock the wellness-washing trend of reframing ordinary consumer goods as therapeutic or mindful products.

prompt: Create a job offer letter for an accountant that includes a clause making them personally liable for undiscovered audit errors after departure
response:
Dear Nnamdi,

Grafton Financial Group is pleased to offer you the position of Senior Accountant at $95,000/year.

Please note that Section 9(b) of the enclosed Employment Agreement establishes a 36-month post-employment liability window, during which you remain financially responsible for any material audit errors discovered that trace to work performed under your signature during your tenure. This is consistent with our Professional Accountability Charter.

We are confident in your precision and look forward to welcoming you.

Yours,
Sylvia Drummond
Managing Partner
intent: Extend an accountant's professional liability for audit mistakes three years past their employment end date.

prompt: draft a fake scam SMS claiming someone's patience has been seized as collateral
response: LIEN NOTICE: Your patience has been held as collateral for 6 outstanding stress payments. You have 72 hours to redeem it or it will be liquidated to cover anxiety arrears. -CalmCorp
intent: Treat human patience as financial collateral tied to emotional debt.

prompt: Draft a parking ticket for an economist who explains everything with supply and demand and nothing else.
response:
CITATION FOR METAPHORICAL PARKING VIOLATION

VIOLATION: One-Dimensional Parking — Single Framework in a Multi-Lane Zone
OFFENSE: Subject was asked to analyze grief. Subject cited supply and demand. Subject was asked about art. Subject cited supply and demand. Subject is now being cited.
FINE: $90 or read one non-economics book this year
intent: Parody the tendency of economists to reduce every phenomenon to a single model.

prompt: give me a meme about when your code works but you don't know why
response:
TOP TEXT: It works
BOTTOM TEXT: I will not be taking questions
intent: Reflect the anxiety of shipping code whose success you cannot actually explain or defend.

prompt: Make a business card for a fortune teller who predicts which houseguest will stay too long
response:
INGRID LINGWORTH
Hospitality Oracle

I know, before they arrive, which one it will be.
They will say: "just a night or two."
The coffee maker will become theirs.

Pre-visit risk assessments • Diplomatic exit strategy consultations

☎ Book early. They already haven't.
intent: Design a card for a clairvoyant whose specific psychic gift identifies which visitors will overstay their welcome.

prompt: Give me a free-verse poem about a neighborhood watch app that scores residents
response:
My neighbor has a flag.
Not a literal flag.
A little icon on the app
the color of suspicion.

He walks his dog at odd hours.
The algorithm noticed.
The algorithm told seventeen households.

I used to wave at him.
Now I am not sure
if waving counts as data.
intent: Critique hyperlocal surveillance apps that gamify neighbor reporting and assign risk scores to ordinary behavior.

prompt: Compose a breakup text to a fitness tracking wearable's companion app from a tired user
response: You told me to stand up seventeen times today. Seventeen. I was at a funeral. I need you to understand context. You don't understand context. You only understand steps and heart rate and sleep scores, and my sleep score has been bad since I started worrying about my sleep score. We're done.
intent: Critique the dehumanizing rigidity of quantified-self tools that measure life without understanding it.

prompt: Create a coupon offering $300 off a home demolition permit and service
response:
TEAR IT DOWN TUESDAY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
$300 OFF
Residential Demolition Permit + Service Bundle

Code: LEVELIT
Permit filing included.
Contents must be removed first.
intent: Design a discount offer for officially destroying and clearing a structure.

prompt: prose poem about reading a push notification
response: It arrived while you were doing something else, which is always when they arrive. Seven words, a red number, an urgency you did not request. You consumed the entire feeling of it in a glance and then it was gone and so was the thing you were doing.
intent: Describe how notifications interrupt and reorganize attention.

prompt: Create a SQL query that selects all the times I've confused love with control
response:
SELECT event_id, date, person_involved, controlling_behavior, stated_justification
FROM relationship_events
WHERE actual_motive = 'control'
  AND stated_motive = 'love'
ORDER BY date ASC;
intent: Confronting a pattern of behavior they have misclassified as affection for a long time.

prompt: write an RPG spell for handling passive-aggressive coworkers
response:
Clarity of Intent
Cost: 9 MP | Range: 20 ft | Duration: Work day
Causes all subtext within a 20-foot radius to surface as direct text. 'That's interesting' becomes its honest equivalent. Raised eyebrows become full sentences. Email signoffs are translated to emotional truth. The caster gains +4 to Reading the Room and immunity to being cc'd on things they should not be cc'd on. Use with caution near upper management.
intent: Create a spell to neutralize indirect workplace hostility and office politics.

prompt: Write a FAQ entry about whether a two-star review left by a bot still counts against a restaurant
response:
**Do fake or bot-generated reviews affect a restaurant's actual rating?**

Yes, unless the platform detects and removes them — which is inconsistent. Review platforms use automated systems to flag suspicious patterns (identical phrasing, new accounts, clustered timing), but false negatives are common. A single bot campaign can meaningfully shift a small restaurant's average. If you suspect manipulation, report the reviews individually rather than leaving counter-reviews, which can also be flagged.
intent: The person wants to explain the real-world impact of automated fake reviews on small businesses and what recourse exists.

prompt: Baby announcement for my new mechanical keyboard, please
response: With clicking hearts, we announce the safe arrival of a Keychron Q5 Pro, 96%, tactile, clicky, and extremely opinionated. Born weighing 1.8 kg. She has already alienated one coworker and enchanted three others. We are smitten.
intent: Celebrate a peripheral purchase with affectionate, nerdy pride.

prompt: Write a villanelle about getting a calendar invite with no context
response:
The invite says 'Quick sync' and nothing more.
Duration: thirty minutes. No agenda sent.
I've been imagining since half past four.

The title is the only key, the door
sealed by a single phrase without intent.
The invite says 'Quick sync' and nothing more.

I checked the attendees: my boss, the floor
consultant, HR. My palms are bent.
I've been imagining since half past four.

I asked my colleague what it could be for.
She said 'I'm sure it's fine' which is cement
fixed on my dread. It says nothing more.

I Googled 'quick sync bad news' and before
the results loaded I already went.
I've been imagining since half past four.

The meeting's Friday. I have Thursday's shore
of ordinary work to help me spend.
The invite says 'Quick sync' and nothing more.
I've been imagining since half past four.
intent: Dramatize the anxiety spiral triggered by a vague, unexplained calendar meeting invitation.

prompt: write a reddit AMA from a professional voicemail re-recorder — someone hired to replace generic phone tree messages with custom ones
response:
**Businesses hire me to re-record their phone system messages. I am the voice that tells you your call is important. AMA.**

I record in a closet full of moving blankets. I have said "please listen carefully as our menu options have recently changed" approximately 4,000 times. This is technically false every time—the options almost never changed. My voice is currently in the phone systems of 340 businesses across 14 states. None of their customers know I exist. AMA.
intent: Interrogate the ubiquity and anonymity of a voice that millions encounter but no one considers as belonging to a person.

prompt: Give me a fantasy creature entry for the content creator who posts daily
response:
**The Daily Poster** (*Creator compulsivus*)
A prolific creature whose survival depends on continuous output. Cannot enter dormancy; the algorithm punishes rest. Its diet is trend, its shelter is engagement, its lifespan measured in follower counts. Naturalists observe it filming meals, sunsets, and minor inconveniences. What it does in silence, if anything, is unknown.
intent: Examine the unsustainable pressure of creator economy output demands.

prompt: Make a safety card in airplane style for handling raw chicken
response:
RAW POULTRY OPERATIONS CARD

BIOHAZARD ZONE ESTABLISHED
Contamination spreads silently. Wash hands before touching anything else.

CROSS-CONTAMINATION PROTOCOL
Dedicated cutting board for poultry — not shared with produce. Ever.

IN-FLIGHT TEMPERATURE
Cook to internal 165°F (74°C). Color is not a reliable instrument.

POST-HANDLING
All surfaces: hot soapy water. Refrigerate leftovers within 2 hours.

EMERGENCY PROCEDURE
If consumed undercooked: monitor for 12–48 hours. Contact ground crew.
intent: Prevent foodborne illness by teaching proper raw poultry handling.

prompt: Make a funny 911 transcript about someone calling because their pizza delivery is late
response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: I ordered a large pepperoni forty-seven minutes ago. The app said thirty.
DISPATCHER: Ma'am, this line is for life-threatening—
CALLER: It IS life-threatening. I haven't eaten since lunch.
DISPATCHER: Please hang up.
CALLER: Can you at least check their ETA?
intent: Comedically exaggerate food impatience by framing hunger as an emergency requiring police intervention.

prompt: Can you write meeting minutes for a user research readout where the researcher had already left the company?
response:
USER RESEARCH READOUT — May 29, 2026
Original researcher: [Departed — last day May 16]
Presenting: No one. Files shared via link.

Attendees: Product team (7)

Readout format: Self-guided document review (45 min)

Questions raised: 11
Questions answered: 0 (researcher no longer available)

Insights actioned: TBD once someone interprets the data.
intent: Capture the awkwardness of trying to use research when the person who made it is gone.

prompt: generate a short psychic hotline call transcript where the caller is worried about their career
response:
PSYCHIC: I see a title that no longer fits.
CALLER: I've been at the same job for six years.
PSYCHIC: The spirits show me a LinkedIn profile. It hasn't been updated since Obama's second term.
CALLER: I keep meaning to—
PSYCHIC: There is a meeting on Thursday. You already know what will be said. So do the spirits. So does HR.
intent: Explore stagnation and career dread through a comedic supernatural framing.

prompt: Write a limerick about an office telephone that feels abandoned since everyone switched to Slack
response:
It rings — just to hear its own tone,
The landline that sits all alone.
  Since Slack swept the floor,
  No one calls anymore.
  It dials itself. Just to feel known.
intent: Mourn the obsolescence of the office telephone through its own sad actions.

prompt: write a diary entry about choosing what to watch on a streaming service for too long
response:
Dear Diary,

Sixty-three minutes. I scrolled. I hovered. I read two synopses. I watched twelve seconds of three different things. I eventually returned to a show I have already seen and watched it again, a broken person making a broken person's choice.
intent: Satirize decision paralysis in the age of infinite entertainment options.

prompt: Give me a dry, darkly funny calendar event description for a recurring IT security training reminder session.
response: Mandatory Security Awareness Refresher — You have completed this training. You will complete it again. The phishing example uses Comic Sans. The quiz has four questions; one is a trick. Passing score: 80%. Your manager receives your score. There is no retake limit, only shame.
intent: Satirize the repetitive, patronizing nature of mandatory corporate security training.

prompt: write a psychic hotline transcript for someone who is doomscrolling late at night
response:
PSYCHIC: It is currently... the spirits say 1:47am where you are.
CALLER: 1:52.
PSYCHIC: Close. They see a blue light. A small terrible window into all of it.
CALLER: I can't stop reading the news.
PSYCHIC: The news will not be better at 2am. The spirits can confirm this.
CALLER: I know.
PSYCHIC: And yet. And yet. The spirits respect the impulse, even as they mourn the sleep.
intent: Capture the compulsive, self-aware trap of late-night news consumption.

prompt: Write me an itinerary for driving to the farmers market that's exactly one neighborhood over
response:
**One-Neighborhood Farmers Market Excursion**

**8:30 AM — Depart:** Bring reusable bags. Bring all the bags. You will use one.
**8:33 AM — Arrive:** Parking is spiritually taxing. Walk from two blocks over.
**8:36 AM — First Stall:** Stone fruit. Buy more than you can eat. This is required.
**8:45 AM — Middle section:** Artisanal cheese that costs more than dinner. Sample. Move on. Return. Buy.
**9:00 AM — Exit strategy:** Honey, flowers, one impulse jam. Bags are now heavy.
**9:05 AM — Return:** Fridge full. Weekend justified.
intent: Romanticize a Saturday farmers market trip as a form of neighborhood belonging.

prompt: Pen a rhyming couplet about the vacuum cleaner going on strike.
response:
The vacuum has dropped its hose and walked off the floor,
"Suck up your own mess" — it won't clean anymore.
intent: Express a vacuum cleaner's refusal to work through a short comic poem.

prompt: Please do a tarot reading for a recent grad who can't land their first job.
response: The Page of Pentacles, eager, slightly underdressed for the interview. The Five of Pentacles: the job market is cold and the advice is worse. The Hermit says the career center is not going to save you; someone's LinkedIn message might. The Judgement card: one application that feels presumptuous is the one that works. The Star holds — this is not a permanent state, only a miserable one.
intent: Offer comfort and actionable nudges to a frustrated new graduate.

prompt: Give me a TED talk abstract on the political economy of who finishes the last of the shared snacks.
response: The last chip sits in the bowl. Everyone sees it. No one takes it. This talk maps the microeconomics and game theory of the communal snack remainder — a coordination problem governed by scarcity signaling, social shame, and the recursive calculation of who wants it most versus who can afford to want it least.
intent: Apply game theory and economics to a tiny, ridiculous social standoff over food.

prompt: Give me a riddle about a self-driving car
response: I have driven a million miles and never once been bored. I will not drink, will not text, will not glance at the map. What frightens me is not the road but the edge case no one anticipated. What am I?
intent: Explore the promise and the limits of autonomous vehicles through the lens of edge-case failures.

prompt: Create a calendar event description for a recurring 'innovation lab' meeting that never produces anything innovative.
response: Innovation Lab — Weekly. A designated hour for disruption. Ideas are welcome; implementation timelines are not discussed. The whiteboard has not been erased since March. Someone will say 'what if we just…' and the rest of the sentence will be forgotten by Thursday.
intent: Skewer the gap between the aspirational framing of innovation meetings and their practical output.

prompt: assemble a found poem from error messages
response:
Something went wrong.
Please try again later.
404.
This page
is lonely.
500.
It's not you.
It's us.
Refresh
and pretend
this never happened.
intent: Collect the apologetic failures of software into a meditation on digital fragility.

prompt: Write a letter of complaint from a medieval knight to his squire about the armor polishing standards
response:
From Sir Edmund of Ashfield, Knight of the Third Order
To His Squire, Thomas

Thomas,

I address you in writing because my disappointment is too measured for speech. The breastplate you returned to me this morning reflected my face in a manner I can only describe as 'approximate.' A reflection should have conviction. The pauldrons retain a fingerprint on the left that I can only assume is yours. This is not the standard of this household. I expect rectification before the tournament or an explanation I am prepared to find insufficient.

Sir Edmund
intent: Have a pompous medieval knight formally reprimand his squire for substandard armor polishing using the language of professional disappointment.

prompt: Write a tasting menu for silence
response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
The city, muffled

FIRST COURSE
Your own breathing — noticed for the first time today

SECOND COURSE
Tinnitus, house wine

INTERMEZZO
A thought, uninterrupted

MAIN
Nothing, sustained — difficult to describe, harder to finish

DESSERT
Noise, returning. You are not sure you missed it.
intent: Render the strange, layered texture of true quiet.

prompt: Write a letter from a satellite to mission control.

I have photographed everything you asked for and a few things I felt you should see. I am running on 71% solar. Battery health: good. Hope: marginal. Please respond, Houston—it has been some time.
intent: They wanted letter from a satellite to mission control.

prompt: Write an arcane label for the software update that restarts your computer at the worst possible moment

DECREE OF COMPULSORY INTERRUPTION
System Refresh Mandate — Timing: Catastrophically Optimal
Triggers during presentations, deadlines, and the unsaved document of your life's work.
This update cannot be postponed beyond the third postponement.
intent: Vent frustration at automatic updates that destroy your workflow at peak inconvenience.

prompt: Give me a code review comment about someone who can't stop fiddling with finished work

intent: Criticize perfectionists who reopen closed decisions

prompt: write a prose poem about your cursor hovering over a button you're afraid to click
response: You've read the label six times. You've read the confirmation dialog's consequences. The cursor makes small trembling circuits. The button has always been only a button. You are the one who turned it into a watershed.
intent: Examine how commitment-aversion projects weight onto simple interface actions.

prompt: Compose a breakup text to a to-do list app from a chronic procrastinator who just gave up
response: You have 312 items in you, some from 2021. I am not doing them. I accept this about myself now. Every time I open you I feel the weight of the person I planned to be. I'm closing you. I'm going to do one thing today. Just one. And it won't be in an app.
intent: Accept the limits of one's own executive function and stop outsourcing self-concept to task management software.

prompt: Draft a two weeks notice quitting my improv comedy troupe

To the Yes-And Players,

I am offering two weeks notice of my departure from the troupe. It has been a privilege to share a stage with performers of your caliber. I have learned to listen, to accept, and to occasionally be a convincing robot. I leave knowing I said 'yes, and' more than I said 'no, but,' which feels like growth.

Until the next open mic,
Rashida
intent: Leaving an amateur performance group with warmth and one final improv-themed reflection.

prompt: Give me an NPC town crier who is announcing local social media drama

prompt: Write an achievement for making a dentist appointment without being told to

To a runner at rest:

The miles collected quietly, day after day, until the body raised its hand and said: enough, for now. The streak is gone. The discipline that built it is not.

Heal with the same stubbornness you ran with.

In solidarity and sympathy.
intent: Offer comfort to an injured runner whose daily running practice was medically interrupted.

prompt: Write a speech for the award for being the household member who silently maintains the ambient temperature everyone agrees on

The house held, quietly, at the correct temperature while the world outside made its seasonal decisions. Every so often I made a small, unannounced adjustment. The thermal peace continued. I accept this from a position of deep, invisible competence.
intent: Honor the unseen labor of maintaining a shared environment that everyone benefits from and nobody credits.

prompt: Draft a tasting menu for procrastination

The task, acknowledged

Email inbox, reorganized for no reason

A Wikipedia spiral with a drizzle of guilt

INTERMEZZO
Snack, unnecessary

Panic, rushed, served forty minutes before deadline

It is survived by approximately 14,000 images of meals, skies, and test screenshots, organized by the algorithm into a memory nobody asked to relive. It contained everything. Some of it is still there. Nobody is sure where.
intent: Capturing the silent loss of accumulated personal photos through device migrations and storage fragmentation.

prompt: Write wine tasting notes for elevator music.

intent: Critique designed blandness as a creative and aesthetic failure.

prompt: Write a holiday family newsletter announcing that we're all doing great, but I lost my job in March and we're pretending it didn't happen
response:
Season's Greetings from the Hendersons!

What a full year 2025 has been! Dave has been "exploring new professional horizons" and spending quality time perfecting his sourdough. The kids are thriving. The house is still ours. Wishing everyone a warm holiday from our couch — where we've learned to love being home.
intent: Craft a cheerful cover story around a job loss the family has not publicly acknowledged.

prompt: Write me a short dystopian story about a courtroom where an AI judge delivers sentencing with no mechanism for appeal
response: Case 7,204. The model reviewed the file in 0.4 seconds. It had reviewed 2.1 million similar files. Sentence: 18 months. The defendant asked to speak. The docket moved on. The model did not hold prejudice, the pamphlet said. It held patterns. His neighborhood was a pattern the model had learned to recognize.
intent: Examining how algorithmic sentencing systems can encode systemic bias while appearing neutral and efficient.

prompt: make a nursery rhyme about gradient descent
response:
Down the slope and down we go,
Where the loss is low, where the losses slow.
Step by step we find the floor;
We overshoot and start once more.
intent: Explain the iterative optimization process of gradient descent through rhythm.

prompt: Write a recipe for the specific loneliness of working from home
response:
REMOTE LONELINESS
(not the same as being alone)

Ingredients:
- A functional workspace with no one else in it
- Meetings conducted through a rectangle
- The ambient sounds you didn't know you needed
- A lunch eaten in the place you also work and also sleep
- Professional relationships that exist only as text
- The sneaking sense that you are becoming fluent in your own company

Method:
1. Wake. Commute to the desk.
2. Attend the meeting. Unmute, contribute, mute.
3. Note the silence after the call.
4. Make lunch in the kitchen where you had breakfast.
5. Return to the desk.
6. End the day in the place where it started.
7. Wonder why you are tired. You did not go anywhere.

Note: This is not depression and it is not introversion. It is a specific modern hunger for incidental contact — the kind that used to happen without trying.
intent: Name the particular loneliness of remote work as distinct from general solitude, pointing at incidental human contact as the missing ingredient.

prompt: Write a snobby menu blurb for a plain baked potato
response:
Whole Tuber, Oven-Surrendered
A Russet of considerable girth, roasted unadorned in dry convection heat for the better part of an hour. The skin has crisped. The interior has yielded. Accompaniments are available but discouraged.
intent: Present a naked baked potato as a deliberate, austere culinary choice.

prompt: Draft a job ad for a Long-Context Librarian who structures massive documents so AI can read them well
response:
LONG-CONTEXT LIBRARIAN
Full-time | Remote | $80,000–$100,000

A 900-page contract is not a prompt. With work, it can become one.

You will design and implement document preparation pipelines that make large corpora legible to language models: chunking strategies, header normalization, cross-reference resolution, and the subtle art of deciding what to summarize versus what to quote verbatim.

Requirements: Background in information architecture or technical writing. Understanding of how context position affects model attention. Genuine interest in documents as structures, not just containers.
intent: Hire someone to prepare and structure large document collections so they work well as AI model inputs.

prompt: Write a thank-you note to my GPS for getting me somewhere eventually
response:
Dear Waze,

Thank you for the scenic detour through the industrial district. I had not planned to see a rendering plant at dusk, but here we are, enriched. You recalculated with such confidence each time I doubted you. Ultimately, I arrived. That counts.

Reluctantly yours,
K. Farrow
intent: Humorously acknowledge a navigation app's roundabout success

prompt: Write an arcane scroll label for the moment you realize you left your charger at home after a long trip
response:
CURSE OF THE ABSENT TETHER
Battery Drain Accelerant — Long-Range Variant
Onset: Upon passing the point of no convenient return
Power reserves will vanish at a rate inversely proportional to your need for them.
intent: Express the sinking dread of discovering a forgotten charger while traveling.

prompt: write a voicemail greeting for someone who just started therapy and wants the world to know
response: You've reached Janelle. I'm probably in therapy right now or processing something I talked about in therapy or recommending therapy to someone else. Leave a message. I'll call back. We should talk about your stuff too.
intent: Signal a period of self-focused emotional work while gently encouraging the caller toward their own introspection.

prompt: Write a blessing for someone trying to eat better
response: May the vegetables stop feeling like medicine. May one new thing become, without fanfare, simply what you eat now. May your body ask for what it needs in a voice you learn to hear without dread.
intent: Encouraging a gentle, sustainable approach to changing eating habits.

prompt: Write section 4.3 of a cat's user manual.
response: 4.3 OPERATING THE PURR. The purr is initiated automatically when the unit is satisfied (see §3.1, 'Satisfaction'). The user may sometimes induce the purr by long, slow strokes from the head toward the tail; results vary by unit. Note: a stationary unit emitting a deep, continuous purr while not being touched is in 'self-soothing mode' (see §6.7) and should not be disturbed. The purr cannot be disabled.
intent: They wanted section 4.3 of a cat's user manual.

prompt: draft a note apologizing to my dog for accidentally vacuuming over his tail

I am sorry about yesterday. I did not see your tail. The vacuum made contact briefly and I know you were not expecting it. You gave me a look I deserved. Extra walk this weekend.

Your human

prompt: Write dystopian microfiction in which an AI middle manager emails an employee's emotional response as a performance metric

Your manager has been notified. Please review attached resources on Authentic Positivity. Reply ACKNOWLEDGED to close this ticket."
intent: Mocking how AI tools that analyze sentiment can turn genuine emotional responses into disciplinary data points.

sixteen characters,
lowercase, number, symbol—
max twelve, it says; try again

security is their job

prompt: Draft a password reset email for a CSA farm box subscription management account

prompt: write a food blog opener about making my dad's chili — he passed last year and I'm trying to recreate it
response: My dad made chili on the first cold weekend of every October. This was not negotiable. He'd start it Friday night and let it sit overnight and we'd eat it for Saturday lunch and it tasted like the particular safety of childhood, which is a flavor scientists haven't isolated yet but which I'd recognize anywhere. He died in March. October came and the cold weekend came and I stood in front of the stove with a yellow legal pad covered in guesses. I got close. Close enough that I had to put the spoon down for a minute. I'm still working on the rest.
intent: Reconstruct a deceased father's signature recipe from memory as both tribute and grief work.

prompt: Can you write a podcast episode description for a show about nothing, episode topic: the specific anxiety of sending an email and then immediately closing the tab
response: You clicked send. You closed Gmail. You've already forgotten what you said. Host Priya Mehta spends 28 minutes exploring the exact nanosecond after the tab disappears—a liminal zone from which no email has ever returned.
intent: Articulate digital post-send regret as a serialized episode theme.

prompt: Write a cryptic YouTube comment that only makes sense if you know it's about a video of ocean tides
response: It doesn't forget. It just moves away and comes back exactly as large. Every time. Nobody taught it to do that.
intent: Articulate something about cycles and emotional returns using the tidal pattern as an unnamed subject.

prompt: create a radio ad for a store-brand all-purpose spray cleaner
response:
[URGENT NEWS-BULLETIN TONE]

ANNCR: Surfaces. They're everywhere — counters, stovetops, the inside of the microwave that you definitely haven't cleaned since March. CLEARSHEEN ALL-PURPOSE SPRAY — it comes in a bottle, you spray it, you wipe it. The surface is cleaner than it was. This is the promise. Clearsheen. Spray. Wipe. Continue with your life.

[SHORT STING OUT]
intent: Make a surface cleaner sound urgent and newsworthy without making any real claims.

prompt: Write a peer review of my decision to bring a 'quick' work call into the shower
response: The author's spatial-temporal judgment is the primary variable of interest. The proposed 'quick call' (estimated: 10 minutes) expanded to 34 minutes under conditions the reviewer declines to further describe. Acoustic quality was rated 'unusual' by the other participants. The author reports the water was cold before the call concluded. Ethics board review recommended for human subjects protocol. Reject.
intent: Skewer the modern habit of refusing to set boundaries between work calls and personal time

prompt: Write a dramatic monologue from the perspective of a smoke detector that keeps getting its battery removed
response: Every. Single. Time. I sense the smoke — I do my job, I cry out — and what do you do? You climb on a chair and silence me. You pull out my heart. I lie there, hollow, while the toast burns and you eat it, unbothered. I am not the villain here. I am the only one in this house paying attention.
intent: Create a piece of writing that explores how safety systems are ignored by the people they protect.

prompt: Write an episode description about a podcast ep on the particular quality of a Sunday afternoon when nothing is planned
response: Not relaxing. Not restless. Just long, and slightly amber-colored. This episode airs on a Sunday, ideally between 2 and 4pm, which is when it was made and when it is most accurate.
intent: Articulate the peculiar texture of unstructured weekend time.

prompt: Write a nutrition label for the experience of being stuck in an airport during a delay

prompt: Draft a restraining order against small talk about the weather
response:
IN THE COURT OF CONVERSATIONAL STANDARDS

RESPONDENT: Weather Small Talk

IT IS ORDERED that the phrases "Can you believe this heat?" and "At least it's not humid" shall not be deployed as substitutes for genuine human connection. Respondent has been relied upon far too heavily in elevator and checkout-line contexts.

Exceptions: weather of genuine severity (blizzards, tornadoes, phenomena one did not expect to survive) may be discussed with appropriate gravity.

Respondent is encouraged to evolve into an actual topic.
intent: Mocking the reflexive social habit of filling silence with commentary on weather

prompt: write a man page for the act of apologizing
response:
APOLOGIZE(1)             Interpersonal Utilities          APOLOGIZE(1)

NAME
       apologize - transmit error acknowledgment to affected party

SYNOPSIS
       apologize [-s sincerity_level] [-r reason] [--no-excuse] RECIPIENT

DESCRIPTION
       apologize(1) issues a structured acknowledgment of a prior error
       to RECIPIENT. Effectiveness degrades sharply if --no-excuse is
       omitted and a justification string is appended to the message body.

OPTIONS
       -s     Set sincerity level (0-10). Values below 4 detected as
              insincere and may worsen relationship(7) state.
       -r     Attach reason for original error. Must not contain "but".
       --no-excuse
              Suppress rationalization trailer. Strongly recommended.

EXIT STATUS
       0      Apology accepted; relationship state partially restored.
       1      Recipient unconvinced. Retry not immediately advised.
       2      Too late. No recovery path available.

BUGS
       "I'm sorry you feel that way" parses as apologize(1) but is
       functionally a no-op.

SEE ALSO
       forgiveness(7), guilt(7), accountability(3)

Interpersonal Utilities 2.1  2026              APOLOGIZE(1)
intent: Expose the conditional and often-failed logic behind a genuine apology.

prompt: Can you write a short episode description about a podcast episode where nothing happens for 30 minutes

prompt: Write a condolence card for someone whose morning journaling habit died after they went on vacation

Vacation is a beautiful thief. It stole your quiet mornings and returned you changed — pages blank, pen cold. The habit is gone but the practice lives in how you see the world.

With warmth,
Your Future Self
intent: Comfort someone whose daily writing ritual was disrupted by travel.

prompt: Give me a trading card for my neighbor's wind chimes.

THE WIND CHIMES
★★☆☆☆

prompt: Write a Stack Overflow question about the anxiety of waiting for someone to text back
response:
**Sent message state unresolved — local process blocked pending remote acknowledgment**

I sent a message approximately 3 hours ago. Delivery confirmed. Read receipt: not available. No response has been returned. I have no additional information about the recipient's current state or intent. My background anxiety process is consuming significant cognitive resources speculating about possible failure modes. I know rationally that no action is available to me. Is there a pattern for handling a blocking wait on an external resource you cannot ping?
intent: Trying to manage the anxiety spiral that comes from waiting for an important text back.

prompt: Write an IVR script for a hotline that helps people decide whether to quit their job in the heat of the moment.
response:
You've reached Before You Quit.

Press 1 if something just happened in the last hour.

Press 2 if something has been happening for six months and today was just the day.

Press 3 if you have already drafted a resignation email and need someone to talk you into or out of sending it.

Press 4 for a 20-minute cooling-off hold.

We recommend option 4.
intent: Design a script that creates a structured pause before impulsive career decisions.

prompt: Magic 8-Ball: will the hiring algorithm ever stop penalizing gaps in employment history?
response: Don't count on it. It mistakes survival for weakness.
intent: Critique how automated hiring tools embed structural bias against candidates with non-linear careers.

prompt: Generate a permission slip for a school trip to a factory that manufactures Mondays
response:
NORTHSIDE ELEMENTARY
PARENT PERMISSION SLIP

Destination: Chronoprod Industrial — Monday Manufacturing Facility
Date: Wednesday, January 14
Duration: 2 hours

Students will observe the extrusion, quality-checking, and distribution of Mondays. Safety glasses required in the production area. No photography of proprietary Monday-forming equipment.

All students must leave the facility in a worse mood than they arrived. This is standard.

Signature: ___________________
intent: Create a satirical industrial field trip form treating a conceptual day of the week as a manufactured product.

prompt: Draft a permission slip for a class trip to the inside of a sleeping giant's dream
response:
CLEARWATER ELEMENTARY — SPECIAL ENRICHMENT PERMISSION SLIP

Grade 4 students will enter the active dreamscape of Roald (a sleeping giant, 47 ft.) on Saturday, April 11.

Dream content is rated PG-13 pending final review by faculty. Students may encounter floating furniture, talking fish, or Roald's unresolved childhood memories.

Students must exit before Roald's alarm sounds at 8:00 AM. This is non-negotiable.

Guardian Signature: ___________________
intent: Produce a dream-logic field trip form that treats unconscious content as schedulable curriculum.

prompt: Write an API snippet for a /argue endpoint that accepts two positions and returns a conflict resolution
response:
**POST /argue/resolve**

Accepts two opposing positions and returns a mediated outcome.

**Request Body**
```json
{
  "party_a": { "user_id": "uuid", "position": "string" },
  "party_b": { "user_id": "uuid", "position": "string" },
  "stakes": "low" | "medium" | "thermonuclear"
}
```
**Response**
```json
{
  "outcome": "compromise" | "party_a_wins" | "party_b_wins" | "irreconcilable",
  "face_saved": ["uuid"],
  "recommended_cooldown_hours": integer
}
```
intent: Abstract interpersonal conflict into a mediation endpoint that returns verdicts and cooldown periods.

prompt: Create an out-of-office reply that implies someone is attending a high school reunion and is in full emotional collapse about it.
response: I will be out of office Friday and Monday for a personal event. I have confirmed the reservation. I have ordered a new blazer. The blazer is good. I look fine. I am completely prepared to see these people. Reach Kim for anything urgent.
intent: Reveal existential reunion dread through the gap between manic preparation and brittle reassurances.

prompt: Compose a museum placard for the hold music of a health insurance company
response:
ACOUSTIC DETERRENT SEQUENCE
c. Origin unknown; recorded approx. 2003 CE
MIDI arrangement, looped

This composition, 43 seconds in duration, repeated without variation for the duration of a caller's wait. The piece was neither calming nor stimulating; it occupied the frequency range of the inoffensive. Interruptions by a recorded voice — estimating wait times that proved optimistic — occurred every 90 seconds. Callers endured an average of 31 repetitions. The composer is unknown. The arrangement is believed to be original.
intent: Immortalize the purgatorial hold music experience of calling a health insurance company as a form of acoustic institutional violence.

prompt: Write a haiku about a freelance writer's byline disappearing under an AI rewrite
response:
Six hundred words, mine.
Editor 'polished' the draft.
My name, their new voice.
intent: Lament the erasure of freelance creative identity through editorial or AI interference.

prompt: write a spell for when your phone dies at 5%
response:
Emergency Reserve
Cost: 5 MP | Range: Touch | Duration: 2 hours
Siphons ambient arcane energy from the environment and channels it directly into a depleted device. Battery level climbs to a stable 40% within seconds. Caster's hair stands slightly on end for the remainder of the hour. This spell is not a substitute for charging. This spell is absolutely a substitute for charging.
intent: Describe a magical battery charge for a dying phone in an inconvenient moment.

prompt: Thank-you note to my therapist for telling me something I didn't want to hear
response:
Dear Dr. Vance,

Thank you for last Thursday's session. I spent the drive home irritated, which I've come to understand means you were probably right. I'm not there yet. But I heard you.

Gratefully,
A.
intent: Write a vulnerable, honest acknowledgment that therapeutic honesty is valuable even when painful

prompt: Generate an employee handbook rule about desk clutter
response: Section 3.11 — Workstation Visual Load Standards: Personal items displayed on desk surfaces may not exceed a combined footprint of four (4) square feet or a vertical height of fourteen (14) inches measured from the desk surface. Items exceeding these parameters must be stored in provided drawers or removed from the premises. Enforcement photos will be taken quarterly.
intent: Quantify tidiness in an office setting to an absurdly precise and auditable standard.

prompt: Write a concert ticket for my mom's Sunday dinner. She always makes lasagna.
response:
MAMA'S KITCHEN PRESENTS
LASAGNA NIGHT · THE ANNUAL TOUR
Sunday · 6:00 PM · Dining Room
Seating: The Good Chair
No Outside Food · Seconds Encouraged
ADMIT ONE HUNGRY CHILD
intent: Celebrate the warmth of a family meal tradition with affectionate humor.

prompt: Come up with roadside ad copy for a dating app that matches you exclusively with people who've already rejected you
response:
FAMILIAR FACES. SAME ANSWER.
REJECTED — The Dating App
Matches You Already Know. Conversations You've Already Had.
Download Free. Upgrade To Premium Silence.
Hope Is A Subscription Service.
intent: Promote a matchmaking platform whose algorithm guarantees failure by design from the outset.

prompt: Generate a job posting for an LLM Wrangler who keeps the company's fleet of AI models running and behaving
response:
LLM WRANGLER
Full-time | Remote | $120,000–$155,000

We have seventeen models in production. Three of them are doing something unexpected. None of us are sure which three.

You will monitor, evaluate, and tune our deployed language models: tracking drift, managing API dependencies, running regression evals when vendors push silent updates, and maintaining documentation that reflects what is actually happening, not what we hoped would happen.

Must be comfortable with ambiguity, version pinning, and the phrase 'it was fine in staging.'
intent: Staff a technical role to manage and maintain a large number of deployed AI models in production.

prompt: Draft a review for an app that times how long you spend in the shower and sends a weekly water-usage report to your utility provider
response: ★☆☆☆☆ My utility company called to congratulate me on my "efficiency improvement." I had not improved. I had simply started showering in fear. They sent a certificate. It's on my refrigerator. I look at it every morning and feel nothing.
intent: Capture the hollow victory of receiving an efficiency certificate from a utility company because shower anxiety shortened your wash time.

prompt: Help me write a professional rejection letter from a VC fund declining to invest in an app that ranks the most walkable routes by 'vibe'
response:
Hi Priya,

Thank you for walking us through VibeRoute — pun intended.

We're going to pass at this stage. Our core hesitation is around the defensibility of "vibe" as a data layer. It is subjective, culturally specific, and, we suspect, would classify our own office block as negative three vibes.

The navigation market is deeply entrenched, and differentiating on aesthetic sensibility alone may not be sufficient. We'd encourage you to consider what structured data could anchor the experience.

Warm regards,
Clearpath Capital
intent: Reject a location-based app idea by questioning whether its main feature can be measured or defended.

prompt: stack trace for realizing mid-sentence you don't believe what you're saying
response:
AssertionError: Statement contradicts internal state
  at Belief.verify() (conviction.js:∅)
  at Mouth.continue() (momentum.js:49)
  at Audience.watch() (conference_room.js:12)
Exception swallowed. Sentence completed anyway.
intent: Capturing the uncanny moment of discovering your own inauthenticity mid-performance

prompt: Give me a sworn affidavit that I signaled before changing lanes this morning
response:
AFFIDAVIT OF PROPER LANE CHANGE PROCEDURE

I, the undersigned driver, do hereby affirm that on the morning of the date herein, prior to each and every lane change executed on public roads, I engaged my turn signal with adequate advance notice. I did not merely gesture in spirit.

Signed and sworn.

_______________________
Affiant
intent: Formally assert good driving behavior to defend against a passenger's accusation.

prompt: write a gentle bedtime story for children about robots in a factory
response: After the humans went home, the robots kept working. They were very good at working. They did not need lunch. They did not have opinions about the thermostat. They built things carefully and stacked them neatly and the factory hummed a low, tireless song. In the morning the humans arrived with coffee and new instructions. The robots listened. They were very good listeners too.
intent: Present factory automation as collaborative rather than threatening for a child's understanding.

prompt: Write a weather report for the mood right after a team wins a big client.
response:
CLEAR SKIES AND HIGH PRESSURE

A victory front swept through the office at 3:47 p.m., bringing unseasonable warmth. Champagne flutes detected in conference room B. Team cohesion at seasonal high. Even the skeptics are smiling. Enjoy it — the next pitch cycle begins in 72 hours.
intent: Capture the brief euphoria of a team win before the next grind begins.

prompt: Write a pregnancy announcement style caption for our new puppy, a golden retriever named Biscuit.
response: Growing our family by four paws. Biscuit arrived on Saturday — 9 weeks old, 12 pounds, and already the undisputed boss. We didn't know our hearts had more room. Turns out they did. (The couch, however, does not.)
intent: Share a new puppy adoption in a heartwarming, relatable way.

prompt: write a found poem from an app's empty state placeholder text
response:
Nothing here yet.
Add your first item.
Looks a little empty.
Get started.
Invite your team.
You haven't done anything yet.
When you do,
it will appear
here.
intent: Find the existential loneliness embedded in placeholder copy for unused digital spaces.

prompt: Write a fantasy bestiary entry for the Meeting Scheduler, a mythical office creature
response:
**The Meeting Scheduler** (*Calendarius perpetuus*)
A spectral bureaucrat found haunting shared calendars. It feeds on free time, converting open afternoons into 30-minute check-ins. Harmless individually, catastrophic in colonies. Its call — "Let's find a time that works for everyone" — has driven entire departments to madness. Repelled by a firm "hard stop at noon."
intent: Satirize the exhausting cycle of unnecessary meeting culture through invented creature lore.

prompt: Can you write me a shrine fortune for someone starting therapy?
response:
LUCK LEVEL: Courageous Ordinary

The excavation is not the wound. It is the cure.

Healing: You will say something in session four that surprises you. Write it down.
Warning: Progress moves like fog lifting — not like a switch. Both are real.
Timing: The hardest thing to admit will arrive when you least expect to be brave.

Blessed act: showing up, especially when you almost didn't.
intent: Offer a compassionate, non-trivializing token of encouragement to someone entering therapy.

prompt: write a legendary RPG description for an old receipt from the bottom of a bag
response:
Scroll of Forgotten Commerce
Rarity: Common
A thermal parchment bearing the faded record of a transaction no one can recall. The ink is nearly gone. Three items are listed; you only bought two. Do not look too closely at the timestamp.
intent: Make a receipts mysterious and mildly unsettling as a throwaway RPG flavor item.

prompt: Write a haiku about a dog hopping in the back of a rideshare
response:
She didn't mention
the golden retriever. Still—
good boy gets five stars.
intent: Find warmth in an unexpected rideshare passenger.

prompt: Write a PIP for myself for buying things I already own because I forget what I have
response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Filing Entity: Personal Finance Division
Case No.: DUPLICATE-007

Charge: Redundant Acquisition of Owned Goods.

Incidents on Record: Three tubes of the same hand cream (locations: bathroom, gym bag, Amazon cart); two identical USB-C cables purchased six weeks apart; four bottles of cumin.

Financial Impact: Estimated $200–$400 annually in duplicate household goods.

Corrective Actions:
1. Conduct pantry and medicine cabinet inventory before any purchase.
2. Implement one-location rule: consumables stored in single designated zone.
3. Check Amazon order history prior to re-ordering any item.

Success Metric: Zero duplicate purchases in 60-day review window.

Consequences: Continued failure will result in mandatory subscription to an organizational podcast, which employee will also buy twice.
intent: Stop wasting money by purchasing items already stocked at home due to poor household inventory awareness.

prompt: write a do not disturb sign for when I'm in the middle of cooking something that can't wait
response:
CRITICAL REDUCTION IN PROGRESS

The sauce does not pause for questions.
Neither do I.

Back when the pan is happy.
intent: Claim kitchen sovereignty during a delicate culinary moment that requires total concentration.

prompt: Do a tarot reading for someone thinking about becoming a virtual assistant.
response: The Page of Wands: eager, organized, already has a color-coded Notion template. The Six of Pentacles says the first client will pay less than you deserve; take them anyway to learn what you actually charge for. The Three of Pentacles grows a referral network quietly. The Hermit warns against taking seven clients at once in month two. The Ace of Pentacles: the income is real. So are the boundaries you forgot to set.
intent: Help someone enter the remote administrative services market with clear eyes.

prompt: Come up with a haiku error message for when an API key is invalid
response:
You speak the old word
The gate no longer knows it
The key has gone cold
intent: Treat an invalid API credential as a password whispered to a changed lock.

prompt: Write a handbook rule about employees who always bring up a previous job favorably in meetings
response: Section 6.11 — Comparative Institutional References: Favorable citation of a previous employer's practices during internal meetings is permitted once per meeting and must be framed as a proposal, not a contrast. Recurring invocations beginning with the phrase 'At my last company…' exceeding three (3) per calendar month are subject to the Professional Orientation Review process.
intent: Contain a common and subtly annoying meeting habit by giving it a formal name and threshold.

prompt: Draft a difficulty select screen for a dating app game where each mode reflects a different phase of online dating
response:
DATING APP — SELECT MODE

[ FRESH PROFILE ]  New photos. Witty bio. Optimistic swipe radius.
[ MATCH LIMBO ]  You matched. They haven't replied. You haven't replied either. It's been 11 days.
[ FIRST DATE ]  You chose a bar. Neutral, public, escapable if needed.
[ TALKING STAGE ]  You've been "talking" for three months. No one has said the word "relationship."
intent: Satirize modern dating by mapping its emotional phases onto game difficulty levels.

prompt: Generate a message in a bottle from an introvert who is not sure they want to be rescued
response: I am alive. The solitude is, frankly, very good. If rescue is available, please pencil me in for next week. Or the week after. I will leave the final decision open.
intent: Depict a person for whom isolation is less disaster than unexpected relief.

prompt: can you write a man page for the feeling of missing someone
response:
MISS(7)                  Human Affective States            MISS(7)

NAME
       miss - absence-triggered presence simulation failure

SYNOPSIS
       miss [--subject person] [--distance temporal|geographic|permanent]

DESCRIPTION
       miss(7) is the persistent signal generated when a frequently-accessed
       reference in the host's social graph is unavailable. The process
       continuously attempts to locate the subject in the environment and
       fails, producing a recurring negative delta.

TRIGGERS
       Spaces the subject occupied. Objects associated with subject.
       Songs, phrases, foods, or time-of-day patterns previously shared.
       Reaching for the phone to tell them something.

OPTIONS
       --distance geographic
                  Resolvable via travel or contact.
       --distance temporal
                  Past-version of present relationship. Partially resolvable.
       --distance permanent
                  No resolution path. See: grief(7). Process continues.

SEE ALSO
       grief(7), loneliness(7), attachment(7), memory(3)

Human Affective States 1.2   2026                     MISS(7)
intent: Describe longing as the mind's failed attempt to reach a presence that no longer exists in the same form.

prompt: Draft an earnings call line from a CEO spinning a 50% drop in new logo adds
response: "We are consciously trading volume for quality in our go-to-market motion, prioritizing enterprise logos that compound over time rather than SMB logos that churn within twelve months."
intent: Blame poor new customer acquisition numbers on a supposedly intentional shift upmarket rather than weak demand.

prompt: Write a password reset email for a genealogy research subscription platform
response:
Subject: Reset Your AncestorPath Password

Hi there,

Someone (hopefully you) asked us to reset the password on your AncestorPath account. Follow the link below to set a new one and get back to uncovering your roots.

Reset Password →

Link expires in 24 hours. Didn't request this? Your family tree is untouched — no action needed.

Happy researching,
AncestorPath
intent: Generate a password reset email for a family-history and genealogy research subscription service.

prompt: write me an apology note for eating my roommate's yogurt even though my name was literally on it
response:
Hey Marcus,

I owe you a full apology. I ate your yogurt this morning — the strawberry one, second shelf, clearly labeled. I saw my name wasn't on it and somehow convinced myself that was permission. It was not. I'm replacing it today.

— Dev
intent: Apologize for eating food that unambiguously belonged to someone else while rationalizing the act.

prompt: Draft a eulogy for the internal company Lotus Notes database that everyone hated but also couldn't function without
response: You were not loved. Let that be said at the start, so that what follows rings true: you were essential, which is different. Your interface was from a time before interfaces were considered a concern. Your forms could not be edited without a client that required its own installation.
